content: Combining Reflexion’s dynamic long-term self-memory framework with Self-Refine’s iterative short-term output feedback loop creates a two-tier self-improvement system for autonomous agents, analogous to how human learners combine daily reflective journaling (stored as long-term memory) with incremental line-by-line editing of specific work outputs. This system lets agents correct both immediate output errors and persistent recurring cognitive biases that only show up across multiple task episodes.
